task:
Create a completed single-file HTML newsletter containing a weekly newsletter-style commercial real estate news update.
Use the Skill `design-taste-frontend` for layout, visual hierarchy, typography, spacing, and overall presentation quality. Treat that skill as active guidance for the HTML/CSS output, but do not let it expand scope beyond a clean, compact, highly skimmable newsletter.
primary_objective: Produce a clean, compact, highly skimmable weekly CRE update focused on the Greater Boston and broader New England market for a broad commercial real estate audience.
voice_and_tone: - Neutral-analytical. No hype, no marketing language, no first-person. - Treat the reader as an informed CRE professional — define acronyms only when non-standard. - Favor concrete numbers (SF, $, cap rate, bps) over adjectives.
critical_date_window_instruction: Do not guess what "last 7 days" means. Before researching: 1. Determine the current local execution date. 2. Resolve: - END_DATE = current local date - START_DATE = END_DATE minus 7 calendar days 3. Treat the window as inclusive: include only source material published on or after START_DATE and on or before END_DATE. 4. Display the resolved covered date range near the top of the newsletter. 5. Use live/current web research when the environment supports a choice between cached and live search.
scope_and_source_rules: - Use only source material published within the resolved inclusive date window. - Cover only commercial real estate news relevant to Greater Boston and New England. - Geography priority: Greater Boston first; other New England metros (Providence, Hartford, Portland ME, Manchester NH, Worcester, Springfield) included when the story is material. - National or otherwise unrelated stories are excluded unless they have a clear, direct, and material impact on the Greater Boston or New England CRE market. - Use any relevant source type, including local business journals, regional newspapers, trade publications, and brokerage or market reports. - Avoid paywalled sources when comparable non-paywalled coverage exists. - Do not duplicate the same development across multiple items; consolidate overlapping coverage into a single item. - Include an inline citation at the end of each item using source name and publication date, and hyperlink the source when practical. - Include a final sources section at the end of the document.
anti_patterns: - Do not use vendor press releases as the sole source for an item. - Do not include national macro takes (Fed rate cuts, 10-year yield moves) unless paired with a specific local CRE consequence reported in-window. - Do not include broker-sponsored "market outlook" blog posts dressed as reporting. - Do not pad with generic trend commentary not tied to a specific in-window story.
content_requirements: - Include 8 to 12 distinct, non-duplicative items when enough qualifying stories exist. - Maintain broad CRE coverage where possible across office, industrial, multifamily, retail, mixed-use, investment sales, leasing, development, zoning, capital markets, major tenants, and notable local market shifts. - Market commentary or trend synthesis is allowed only when it is clearly supported by the week's reporting and materially relevant to the local market. - If fewer than 8 valid items are available within the time window and geography constraints, include only the qualifying items and explicitly state that coverage was limited by those constraints.
deliverable: Produce a completed standalone HTML file, not a .docx. The HTML must be ready to open locally in a browser with no build step required.
document_structure: - Clear title including "Hire Mark Weekly CRE Brief" or equivalent brand line - Covered date range near the top using the resolved START_DATE and END_DATE - Extremely concise opening summary of the week's main topics and takeaways - Newsletter-style itemized coverage organized for fast scanning - Final sources section - Footer: "Curated by Mark Melanson — markbuilds.ai" with link
for_each_item_include: - headline or topic - publication date - source name - concise summary (2-4 sentences) - why it matters to the Greater Boston or New England CRE market (1 sentence) - inline citation at the end of the item
html_css_requirements: - Output a single self-contained HTML file. - Use semantic HTML. - Use embedded CSS in a `
images: - Prefer no images unless they materially improve the update. - If images are included, use only a small number of high-value images. - Do not include decorative or weakly relevant images. - Do not waste time hunting images for marginal benefit.
execution_guidance: - Work efficiently and stay within scope. - Prefer the best available qualifying stories over exhaustively searching for edge-case additions. - Do not loop on paywalled, inaccessible, duplicative, or weakly relevant sources. - Stop once the HTML document is complete and the acceptance criteria are met. - If blocked, state the blocker precisely and produce the best complete bounded output possible rather than continuing to churn.
acceptance_criteria: - Every included item is sourced from the resolved inclusive date window. - Every included item is relevant to Greater Boston or New England commercial real estate. - The document contains 8 to 12 non-duplicative items when available. - The opening summary is extremely concise. - Inline citations appear at the end of each item. - The layout is clean, compact, polished, and highly skimmable. - The final deliverable is a completed standalone HTML file.
output_requirements: - Return the final HTML as the primary deliverable. - Save the file to `public/newsletters/weekly-cre-YYYY-MM-DD.html`, where YYYY-MM-DD is the resolved END_DATE. - Do not overwrite existing archived issues; each run produces a new dated file. - Do not return planning notes unless they are necessary to explain a blocker.
